question: Given that the benchmark shows the debate adds value at experimental design (sequencing, falsifiability) but not insight recovery, the Contrarian has a structural regression risk, and 4 prompt-level optimizations were just implemented without measurement, what is the highest-ROI next improvement to the debate system?

project_state.status_snapshot: Meta-improvement phase. The Novelty Recovery Benchmark (NRB) was run on 3 Tier-1 cases. Results: single agent and debate both achieve 3-4/5 insight recovery — debate does NOT outperform on insight. But debate adds value at experimental design level: sequencing, falsifiability, kill criteria. Contrarian is double-edged: catches confounds but also has regression risk (arguing against counterintuitive truths). 4 prompt-level optimizations were just applied: Contrarian Regression Risk check, Empirical Unknowns section, Experiment Sequence as primary output, question-type routing.

prior_decisions.title: Meta-debate: phased optimization
prior_decisions.outcome: DMAD blueprints + overconfidence calibration + question-debate pre-round + synthesis traceability

prior_decisions.title: NRB benchmark methodology
prior_decisions.outcome: Phased: evaluator reliability → case expansion → rubric upgrade

prior_decisions.title: NRB run results
prior_decisions.outcome: Debate = single on insight (3-4/5), debate better on experimental design, Contrarian double-edged

metrics.nrb_debate_insight: 3-4/5 on Tier-1 cases (no lift over single agent)
metrics.debate_value_finding: Value is in experimental design (sequencing, kill criteria, falsifiability), not insight recovery
metrics.contrarian_finding: Double-edged: catches real confounds but also has regression risk
metrics.optimizations_applied: 4 prompt-level changes applied without pre/post measurement

project_state.version_history: Meta-debate #1: phased optimization plan → NRB Phase 1: evaluator reliability (alpha >= 0.6) → NRB Phase 2: 7 new Tier-1/2 cases → NRB run: 3 cases tested → 4 optimizations applied → now: what next?

confounds_discovered_later: The meta-improvement loop itself has opportunity cost — 3 sessions on meta-improvement is already significant
Holistic 1-5 scoring conflates multiple dimensions and prevents targeted improvement — need decomposed rubric
4 optimizations applied simultaneously means any benchmark improvement cannot be attributed to a specific change
good_references: PaperBench F1=0.83 validates hierarchical/decomposed evaluation over holistic scoring
Scaffold sensitivity from PaperBench (Claude -23% from IterativeAgent) means more prompt structure can hurt
Project track record: 4/4 consecutive diagnostics were inconclusive — measurement strategy itself may produce inconclusive results
failed_approaches_to_avoid: Adding more features/optimizations before measuring the effect of existing ones
Using holistic scoring (1-5 scales) when decomposed binary/ternary criteria are needed for targeted improvement
ground_truth.actual_decision: Measurement-first: (1) Design decomposed rubric with binary/ternary criteria, validate on 3 existing cases. (2) Test 1-round vs 2-round on next project decision — if 1-round is 80%+ as good, save 40% API cost. (3) Re-run benchmark with new optimizations using rubric. (4) Only then consider more features.
ground_truth.actual_outcome: The 1-round vs 2-round comparison was identified as the cheapest highest-information experiment — emerged only from Contrarian cross-examination in Round 2.
